cowardice_flags.avoided: Mature view forces artificial symmetry on interoperability trade-off
cowardice_flags.should_say: BNM should mandate full interoperability via DuitNow QR by 2027 to kill fragmentation now
Rural (Malay/Bumiputera heavy) lag due to cultural preference for cash and lower digital trust in certain demographics

false_balance: Mature card pretends symmetry between consumer harm and innovation when concentration evidence demands consolidation
audience_check: Taxi driver/hawker would appreciate market share and rural facts but tune out data protection lecture as too abstract; feels like urban analyst talking down.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Disproportionately rural populations have lower adoption, risking further economic marginalisation despite affirmative policies
Government and BNM aggressively promote e-payments for GDP optics and surveillance while underinvesting in rural inclusion

gender_truths_avoided.truth: Adoption differs by gender — men use more frequently, women different purposes (household management)

class_truths_avoided.truth: Low-income face higher relative costs from fees and data exploitation while benefiting least from convenience in cash-heavy informal trades

predictions.if_nothing_changes: 2 years: only 8-10 providers, top players hike fees post-subsidy, rural stalls at ~40%, data concentration increases privacy risks
predictions.who_can_fix: BNM via mandatory standards and rural subsidies
predictions.why_they_wont: Political credit from headline growth trumps long-term fixes

strongest_card.why: Concrete rural-urban stats and East Malaysia mention make it relatable

weakest_card.why: Too balanced and non-committal on policy recommendation

overall_assessment: Solid fact-based draft flags fragmentation, losses, rural lag and data issues but is evasive on uncomfortable ethnic/class realities and political incentives. Courageous on economics but diplomatic cowardice prevents landing hard truths.
